articles.title: NATIONAL STANDARD: Beyond ‘best before’ — Sabs unveils landmark draft plan to tackle food waste crisis
articles.summary: ‘Best before’ is not ‘use by’. That distinction, long misunderstood across South Africa’s food system, is one of the central clarifications in Sans 2088, the country’s first national framework for food donation and redistribution.

articles.title: Parliament’s failure has allowed corruption to flourish and left a nation betrayed
articles.summary: The Zondo and Madlanga Commissions have shown that South Africa’s accountability architecture has been compromised and criminal syndicates have taken full advantage.

articles.title: June 16 was the invoice for our democracy — 50 years on, it remains unpaid
articles.summary: The road to 27 April 1994, Freedom Day in South Africa, started on the dusty streets of Soweto on 16 June 1976. Had Hector Pieterson not been shot dead that morning he would be a relatively young 62 today. That fact alone – 50 missed years of living – tells us how much he and other youngsters sacrificed in the service of freedom.

articles.title: DISASTROUS START: Bafana Bafana’s opening match drought in World Cups continues after Mexico horror show

articles.title: THE HOLE STORY: ‘Premature and irresponsible’ — No date set to fix collapsing Nelson Mandela Bay roads
articles.summary: The Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality said this week that it would be ‘premature and irresponsible’ to set a date for fixing two of the metro’s busiest roads, the William Moffat Expressway and Kabega Road, as officials have indicated they are awaiting ‘potential’ funding from the national government.

articles.title: CLASSROOM VIOLENCE OP-ED: We banned corporal punishment but haven’t taught teachers what to replace it with
articles.summary: Even after its banning, corporal punishment is still being used in some schools and for a new generation of teachers they need to learn to exert discipline in classrooms without the use of the rod.

articles.title: HIGHER EDUCATION: Deputy minister proposes replacing NSFAS with direct Treasury funding
articles.summary: As NSFAS enters its third period under administration, Deputy Minister Mimmy Gondwe has proposed replacing the embattled scheme with direct National Treasury funding to save costs and improve accountability.

articles.title: JOBURG MASSACRE: After mass shooting, Cleveland residents fear what will happen when SAPS leaves
articles.summary: Residents of the Jumpers informal settlement in Cleveland, Johannesburg, describe how they’ve lived in fear for years, long before a massacre claimed 12 lives.

articles.title: HAZARDOUS OPERATIONS: Unsafe manganese dust levels trigger health and legal warnings in Nelson Mandela Bay
articles.summary: Manganese dust has so thoroughly contaminated the air over Nelson Mandela Bay’s harbour and Markman Industrial area that residents are being exposed to levels far beyond legal limits — and city officials knew about it for months without warning them.

articles.title: TEAM GUIDES — GROUP I: French favourites to top ‘Group of Death’, while Norway, Senegal and Iraq pose threats
articles.summary: Having reached the last two Fifa World Cup finals, France are the favourites to top their group. They will face stiff tests from Norway and Senegal. The fourth team in the group, Iran, will be extra-motivated on the back of off-field issues involving their country and the tournament co-host, the US.

articles.title: LOCAL ELECTIONS 2026: The battle for Joburg: ANC weighs Chikane, Makhura and Masuku to take on Zille
articles.summary: With Johannesburg’s future hanging in the balance, the ANC is considering three very different contenders – Frank Chikane, David Makhura and Loyiso Masuku – as it searches for a heavyweight capable of stopping Helen Zille’s bid for the country’s biggest city.

articles.title: FOOT-AND-MOUTH DISEASE: False metric: Government confusing vaccination target with population immunity
articles.summary: Eighty percent vaccinated over a year is not the same as 80% immune at a point in time. Only a second booster can stop foot-and-mouth disease.

articles.title: MUNICIPAL PARALYSIS: Nelson Mandela Bay needs 101 remedies, but will anyone implement them?
articles.summary: Resistance from within Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality is hampering efforts to implement 101 urgent corrective measures identified by an intervention team, raising fresh concerns about the metro’s ability to recover from years of governance failures.

articles.title: BREAKDOWN: RAF medico-legal experts owed more than R121m as claims system buckles
articles.summary: The Road Accident Fund’s medico-legal system has been described in Parliament as a web of poor planning, unpaid invoices, procurement confusion and broken accountability, with some medical experts waiting years to be paid for work already used in court.

articles.title: AFTER THE BELL: Hot listing summer returns after a long private market freeze-out
articles.summary: The AI companies are coming for public money to fund the next phase of whatever they are building for the future of humanity.

articles.title: URBAN REGENERATION: Forgotten Bellville is finally getting an overdue ‘glow-up’
articles.summary: The Greater Tygerberg Partnership has convinced the City of Cape Town to stand behind a public-private initiative to illuminate the northern suburban area.

articles.title: PERSONAL FINANCE: Why vehicle insurance premiums differ from one person to the next
articles.summary: Factors like age, claims history and even where a vehicle is parked can affect the premium.

articles.title: INTO THE CATHEDRAL: Echoes of the past: Bafana follow the footsteps of Pelé and Maradona
articles.summary: Sometimes it’s as much about the “where” as the “what”. And tonight, at 9pm SA time, Bafana Bafana will take to the pitch at the magnificent and iconic Azteca Stadium in Mexico City to face the hosts in the opening game of the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Youth Day in Soweto: Your must-do June 16 guide
articles.summary: It’s been 50 years since the 1976 Soweto uprising, and this weekend Jozi My Jozi invites people to spend a symbolic 76 hours in Soweto. We’ll be spending the weekend there and hope you will join us.

articles.title: EDUCATION REFORM: Government phases out outdated qualifications to boost graduate employability
articles.summary: In a bid to improve graduate employability and align education with workplace needs, the Department of Higher Education and Training has reviewed 1,475 pre-2009 “legacy” qualifications as part of a major transition towards occupational qualifications that include practical work experience.

articles.title: RACE READY: Comrades marks 50th edition of ‘up run’ with big focus on safety
articles.summary: The 50th Comrades “up run” will be the shortest in recent history, with organisers introducing expanded safety measures for the country’s most iconic race.

articles.title: Military helicopter crash in Pakistani Kashmir killed 22, security sources say
articles.summary: At least 22 personnel were killed in Wednesday’s military helicopter crash in Pakistani Kashmir, security sources said on Thursday, as funeral processions were held in the territory’s capital Muzaffarabad.

articles.title: Sweden drops plan to lower age of criminal responsibility to 13 years old
articles.summary: Sweden’s right of centre government will drop its controversial plan to cut the age of criminal responsibility to 13-years-old from the current 15-years-old, it said on Thursday.

articles.title: Ten-year-old American fashion designer shuttles between school and runways
articles.summary: At four years old, Max Alexander told his parents he was a dressmaker. By age 10, he was showing a collection at Paris Fashion Week.

articles.title: India monsoon slows; below-average rain seen over next two weeks
articles.summary: India is expected to receive below-average rainfall over the next two weeks, especially in central and northern regions, as “western disturbances” have slowed the progress of the annual monsoon, two senior weather bureau officials said on Thursday.
